other_observers: Tom McConnell - Glenwood Springs; Vic Zerbi - Glenwood Springs
description: Dark brown head, all black bill-upper and lower. Dark eye with NO EYE RING. Dark head contrasted with a lighter dark brown back. Tail and wings dark brown, no WING BARS and no white observed in the tail feathers. First day of observation was overcast and the underside of the bird from below the bill to and including the under tail coverts were white. A slight was of gren-gray merged out from the top of the wings on top of the chest. Light was better on 12/14 and the white under parts appeared to have a light was of yellow. A Song Sparrow perched next to the bird and gave good looks for size comparison. The Phoebe was slightly larger looking, but similar in size.Behaviors: The bird used open conspicuous perches at the shrub vegetation height (~4'). Posture was erect when perched; bill angle was tilted slightly upward. Bird constantly pumped its' tail downward. It often gave a call note from the perch. It flew down often to feed on mats of watercress for long periods of time with Song Sparrows.Call: The bird was "talkative"; it never gave a primary song but called often. The call was a single, loud. clear "CHIP". The call was clearly audible at 50 meters or more. It was a crisp, single not repeated often on both days.Plumage: basic
